item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think the many clearance levels are a bit unnecessary. Redacting information about SCP-5916-10 is fine, but the first two collapsibles ruin the reading flow a bit, at least for me.

This post is great. I just wanted to point something out:

5916-8: Considered neutralized in 19██ when it suddenly dove downward at attacking speed and Foundation observers lost visual. Hydrophones reveal 5916-9 suddenly disappearing almost 1km from the seabed.

You're talking about SCP-5916-8, yet you refer to him as 5916-9.
Great inversion of a classic SCP joke.

item.title: Operational Staff Post
item.content:encoded: Image is All Rights Reserved, which is not compliant with CC BY-SA 3.0. As such, it has since been removed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I kept waiting for shark punching center to be referenced. Perhaps they were responsible for the sharks having a grudge against boxers. You played it straight instead and I respect that.

item.title: Re: Site Crit Team
item.content:encoded:
Thanks for the input! All really valid suggestions; I think I'm probably gonna have to develop -10 more anyway, because looking at it now he seems more like an addendum than something that's kind of central to the SCP, which is sort of the goal here.

EDIT: Also, forgive me but I've been off the site for a while, so SP&G?

item.title: Mod Post - Info - Open
item.content:encoded: JellyfishesKeeper, don't add tags to articles if you don't know what you're doing. You didn't tag this article properly and staff had to fix it.

item.title: Site Crit Team
item.content:encoded:
Hello author,

I'm going to just rapid-fire some thoughts here...
Return

This is kind of fun and there's definitely something here but the sum total of the details is just lacking a bit of cohesion for me. It doesn't quite "come together" if you will.

For starters, stuff like…

approximately 560.5km in diameter.

You're talking about an area, and then to say approximately but go to the ".5" at this level seems silly.

There's just a general lack of precision in the language throughout this piece that, if corrected, would cement this a lot. The big table in the Description is more or less useless with all the black boxes. All you wanted to convey was which ones are neutralized, you didn't need to gum up the screen with a big patch of redacted info.

Three minutes after a boxer has entered its region of the ocean, SCP-5916 will become hostile and immediately accelerate towards the intruder at speeds in excess of Mach 20.

Three minutes? Why get specific here? The fact that it's 3 minutes doesn't seem to actually matter. You can just say "shortly after" and we come out the same on the other side.

I'd also point out that moving through the water at Mach 20 would kill every living thing in the ocean across the entirety of planet Earth. Mach 20 is so inconceivably fast that I'm not sure you understand. Traveling at Mach 20 would allow a person to circumnavigate the entire planet in less than 2 hours. I think you're going for "really fast" here, and in your rush to convey that you've introduced something so hyperbolic my suspension of disbelief is shattered.

Dr. Yung: You mean 5916-9?

5916-10: Little punk thought he could take me with a sucker punch. Showed him.

How does this -10 know who -9 is? That's our designation not theirs.

Too many errors here; this could do with a few more passes for critique and SP&G. You're 80-90% of the way there in my estimation.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Added some tags, you can change it if needed

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Image is borrowed from:
Original Source: http://www.mcdoa.org.uk

http://www.mcdoa.org.uk/Bang.htm

Two things:

1.) I've been sitting on this SCP since roughly 2016, but only until now did I kind of clean it up enough to be presentable. Ran it past critique last year and incorporated all the criticisms into the final product, so hopefully, this turns out decently.

2.) Yes, this SCP is referencing exactly what you think it is.
